breaking.title: At least 35 dead after major earthquake strikes southern Philippines
breaking.summary: The magnitude-7.8 quake triggered small tsunami waves in the Philippines, Indonesia and Japan.

breaking.title: 7 Indian workers killed, 5 critically injured in road accident in Dubai
breaking.summary: The accident resulted in seven fatalities and nine injuries, including five serious and four moderate injuries, the Dubai Police said, while adding that all injured were transported to the hospital for treatment

breaking.title: Iran and Israel say they have halted strikes after first exchange of fire since truce
breaking.summary: Iran launched about 30 missiles at Israel following a strike in Lebanon, while Israel carried out two waves of air strikes in Iran.

breaking.title: Naga Villager Killed In Attack By Suspected Insurgents In Manipur
breaking.summary: A Naga villager was shot dead by suspected insurgents in Manipur

breaking.title: Stock market jitters remain amid tech fears and renewed Middle East attacks
breaking.summary: Markets in Asia are hit by a tech sell-off, and oil is volatile as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

breaking.title: Kremlin says Macron, Merz and Starmer talk of peace, but help Kyiv with new weapons to continue the war
breaking.summary: The leaders of ‌Britain, Germany ​, and France said on June 7 they supported a proposal ‌for talks between Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy and Russian President ‌Vladimir Putin to ​try to secure a ceasefire, ⁠and that ⁠Europe would play a ‌role

breaking.title: US judge strikes down Donald Trump's $100k visa fee, calls it unlawful
breaking.summary: A US federal judge has ruled against a $100,000 fee imposed by former President Donald Trump on new H-1B visas. The judge found the fee unlawful. This fee significantly increased the cost for highly skilled foreign workers seeking to enter the United States. The ruling came after a lawsuit filed by state attorneys general.

breaking.title: US warplane strikes, disables oil tanker near Oman; crew of 24 Indians evacuated
breaking.summary: An unladen oil tanker, MT Marivex, carrying 24 Indian crew members, was disabled and caught fire in the Gulf of Oman following a US military action. The US stated an F/A-18 Super Hornet fired a precision munition after the vessel allegedly violated a blockade of Iran's ports and ignored directions. All Indian seafarers were safely evacuated with Omani assistance.
